title: Dichanthelium davidsei
taxonomy: Dichanthelium davidsei is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a type of wild grass that is native to North America. The classification of Dichanthelium davidsei is further divided into the genus Dichanthelium, tribe Poeae, subfamily Pooideae, and class Liliopsida. The species is also known as David's rosette grass or David's panic grass. Dichanthelium davidsei is a relatively rare species of grass that is found in specific regions with unique soil and climate conditions.

intro: Dichanthelium davidsei, also known as David's rosette grass, is a perennial grass species native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and improve overall immune function. Bioactive constituents of Dichanthelium davidsei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its potential therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Dichanthelium davidsei include tea infusions, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support joint health and immune system function.
